title: Problems mapping ports inside pod
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I am trying to create a pod, in which I want to create 2 containers in, where one is a nginx proxy listening on ports 80 and 443 and the other container should only talk to the proxy internally (in the pod) and should not listen to the "outside."

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
# podman pod create -p 80:80 -p 443:443 --name=bitwarden_pod
15e6d7e90ed10afcbf3dccb6fe31180b0635e57aed7584ceaa381c468b8e17fd
```

```
# podman run --name=nginx_proxy -p 80 -p 443 --pod=bitwarden_pod -d bitwardenrs_nginx_proxy
cannot set port bindings on an existing container network namespace
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       1.0.2-dev
Go Version:    go1.11.5
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

